articleSummary:
**Summary:**  
The article argues that AngularJS (v1.x) performance degrades in medium-to-large apps due to overuse of `$scope`, `$watch`, and directive nesting, leading to browser slowdowns from excessive DOM recompilation. It advocates for disciplined use of 1-way binding, minimizing directive depth, and leveraging Browserify for modular code, while warning against "AngularJSification" of every problem. Alternatives like ReactJS and framework-agnostic JavaScript approaches are suggested for projects needing lightweight, component-driven solutions. The tone is practical, blending tutorial-style tips with critical analysis of AngularJS anti-patterns, using metaphors like "2-way data binding as a two-way sword" to highlight trade-offs. Intended for AngularJS developers seeking performance optimization strategies.
